metadata.title: Implement Scoring Algorithm and Weakness Identification

story.iWant: my results analyzed to identify specific weak areas

tasks.task: Implement generateWeights() pure function with normalization
Write comprehensive unit tests with 100% coverage

criterion.description: Accuracy Scoring per Domain - Number Sense Score: (Q1-Q4 correct / 4) × 5 = 0-5 scale
Accuracy Scoring per Domain - Spatial Awareness Score: (Q5-Q7 correct / 3) × 5 = 0-5 scale
Accuracy Scoring per Domain - Operations Score: (Q8-Q10 correct / 3) × 5 = 0-5 scale
Weakness Identification - Score ≤ 2.5 = Weak area (needs priority training)
Weakness Identification - Score 2.6-3.5 = Moderate (needs some focus)
Training Plan Weight Generation - Weak areas get 2x weight, Moderate areas get 1x weight, Strengths get 0.5x weight
Training Plan Weight Generation - Normalize weights to sum to 1.0 (avoid floating point errors)
Assessment record saved to Dexie assessments table with all fields
Training plan weights stored in SessionContext for use by Training epic

section.name: Data Models and Contracts
APIs and Interfaces
Workflows and Sequencing
section.content: 5. Calculate Scores - ScoringService.calculateDomainScore() for each domain - ScoringService.identifyWeaknesses() - ScoringService.generateTrainingWeights() ↓ 6. Display Results - ResultsSummary shows strengths (green), weaknesses (orange/red) - Training plan weights stored in SessionContext - Full assessment record saved to Dexie (status: completed)

section.name: Question Sequencing
section.content:
- Questions 1-3: Number Sense (quantity comparison, number line x2) - Questions 4-6: Spatial (rotation x2, mirror recognition) - Questions 7-10: Operations (basic ops x2, estimation x2)
NOTE: Story 2.5 uses a DIFFERENT question distribution: - Questions 1-4: Number Sense (4 questions) - Questions 5-7: Spatial (3 questions) - Questions 8-10: Operations (3 questions)

section.name: Service Layer Patterns
section.content: - Pure functions preferred for services - No side effects in calculation/transformation logic - Service modules export named functions, not classes - Location: src/services/{domain}/{module}.ts - All services must have corresponding .test.ts files - 100% test coverage enforced by CI

section.name: Testing Standards
section.content: - Framework: Vitest 3.2.4 - Component tests: React Testing Library 16.3.0 - Coverage: 100% statement/branch/function/line - Pattern: AAA (Arrange, Act, Assert) - File naming: {module}.test.ts or {Component}.test.tsx - Location: Co-located with source files

artifact.description: Assessment schema interface (Epic 1) - NOTE: This is a simpler schema than tech-spec. Story 2.5 implements the simpler version.
Dexie database singleton and operations pattern
Question generation patterns with domain categorization
Result interface pattern from implemented questions

constraints.constraint: Pure functions only - no side effects in scoring logic
Export named functions: calculateDomainScore, identifyWeaknesses, generateWeights
Weight normalization must sum to exactly 1.0 (avoid floating point errors)
Use simpler Assessment schema from schemas.ts, not the complex tech-spec version
Scoring must execute in <50ms for all 10 questions
Local-first - no external APIs, all data in Dexie

interface.name: calculateDomainScore
interface.description: Calculates domain score on 0-5 scale based on question distribution: - Number Sense: Q1-Q4 (4 questions) → (correct / 4) × 5 - Spatial: Q5-Q7 (3 questions) → (correct / 3) × 5 - Operations: Q8-Q10 (3 questions) → (correct / 3) × 5

interface.name: identifyWeaknesses
interface.description: Categorizes domains based on score thresholds: - Score ≤ 2.5 → Weak area (needs priority training) - Score 2.6-3.5 → Moderate (needs some focus) - Score > 3.5 → Strength (occasional practice)
interface.returns: Object with arrays of domain names by category

interface.name: generateWeights
interface.description:
Generates normalized training weights: - Weak areas: 2.0x weight - Moderate areas: 1.0x weight - Strengths: 0.5x weight - Normalize so sum of all weights = 1.0
Example: - number_sense: weak (2.0) - spatial: strength (0.5) - operations: moderate (1.0) - Sum: 3.5 → Normalize: [2.0/3.5, 0.5/3.5, 1.0/3.5] = [0.571, 0.143, 0.286]
interface.returns: Object mapping domain names to normalized weights (sum = 1.0)

interface.name: Dexie Assessment Operations

standards.standard: 100% statement, branch, function, and line coverage required
Vitest framework with AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert)
Test pure functions in isolation (no mocking needed for calculation logic)
Use fake-indexeddb for Dexie integration tests (if needed)
Clear test descriptions: "should calculate correct score for X questions correct"

category.name: calculateDomainScore Edge Cases
category.idea: All questions correct (4/4, 3/3) → should return 5.0
All questions incorrect (0/4, 0/3) → should return 0.0
Partial correct - number sense: 2/4 → should return 2.5
Partial correct - spatial: 2/3 → should return 3.333...
Partial correct - operations: 1/3 → should return 1.666...
Empty questions array → should return 0.0 (or throw error?)
Questions from wrong domain filtered out correctly

category.name: identifyWeaknesses Threshold Testing
category.idea: Score exactly 2.5 → should be classified as weak (≤ 2.5)
Score 2.6 → should be classified as moderate
Score 3.5 → should be classified as moderate (≤ 3.5)
Score 3.6 → should be classified as strength (> 3.5)
All domains weak → weaknesses array has all 3 domains
All domains strong → strengths array has all 3 domains
Mixed distribution → verify correct categorization

category.name: generateWeights Normalization
category.idea: All weak (2.0 × 3 = 6.0 total) → each should be 1/3 ≈ 0.333
All strong (0.5 × 3 = 1.5 total) → each should be 1/3 ≈ 0.333
All moderate (1.0 × 3 = 3.0 total) → each should be 1/3 ≈ 0.333
Mixed (weak=2.0, moderate=1.0, strength=0.5, sum=3.5) → [0.571, 0.286, 0.143]
Sum of all weights MUST equal 1.0 (use toBeCloseTo for floating point)
No negative weights
No weights > 1.0
Empty categories → should handle gracefully (or throw error?)

category.name: Integration with Assessment Schema
category.idea: Result object matches Assessment interface fields (weaknesses, strengths arrays)
Domain names use correct format: "number_sense", "spatial", "operations"
Weights can be stored as JSON in assessment record

category.name: Performance
category.idea: Scoring 10 questions completes in < 50ms
No unnecessary iterations or O(n²) operations
